title: elaphoglossum inaequalifolium
intro: Elaphoglossum inaequalifolium, also known as Inequifolium, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to Central and South America, where its rhizome and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, providing relief from pain and inflammation, as well as promoting wound healing. The leaves and rhizomes of the Elaphoglossum inaequalifolium plant can be prepared as an infusion or tincture for its medicinal use.
